title: aquilegia coerulea var. chrysantha
intro: Aquilegia coerulea var. chrysantha, commonly known as the golden columbine, is a variety of the columbine plant that is native to the mountainous regions of North America. The golden columbine has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that have been shown to have antimicrobial and antiviral effects. The health benefits of the golden columbine are attributed to its ability to support respiratory health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the golden columbine can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
